steps.action: Build the public `@shuvdex/git-ops` package surface, provision an isolated divergent origin/repo-under-test/peer sandbox under `/tmp/shuvdex-user-testing-val-git-010-round3` on `shuvtest`, and set `git config pull.rebase false` in `repo-under-test`.
steps.expected: The repo-under-test is one local commit ahead of `origin/main` with no existing merge state so a subsequent pull will exercise a real content-conflict path.
steps.observed: Setup completed successfully: `repo-under-test` local HEAD was `0bc6f11df855609ce5ed7adc3eeffb014c6b719d`, peer HEAD was `c184dbea6c461ff330dd05859bd14337719fec14`, tracking `origin/main` in `repo-under-test` remained `be51c3dcfbef086b4ff29a9928c61bb1387ecf93` before the API call, and `git status --short --branch` showed `## main...origin/main [ahead 1]`.

steps.action: Call `GitOps.pull(host, repoPath)` through the public package API via `node --input-type=module` from the repo root.
steps.expected: The API returns a typed `MergeConflict` error whose `files` list includes the conflicted path (`conflict.txt`).
steps.observed: The API returned `MergeConflict` after 597 ms with conflicted files ['conflict.txt']. Error message: `Merge conflict on shuvtest: conflict.txt`.

steps.action: Verify the repository directly with raw git/SSH after the API call and check recoverability.
steps.expected: Direct git reports the same conflicted files as the API, leaves the repo in a recoverable merge-conflict state immediately after pull, and `git merge --abort` restores a clean non-merge state without changing local HEAD.
steps.observed:
Direct verification showed status `## main...origin/main [ahead 1, behind 1]
UU conflict.txt`, conflicted files ['conflict.txt'], `MERGE_HEAD_PRESENT=yes`, `git merge --abort` exit `0`, post-abort HEAD `0bc6f11df855609ce5ed7adc3eeffb014c6b719d`, and post-abort status `## main...origin/main [ahead 1, behind 1]`.

summary: Tested 1 assertion: 1 passed, 0 failed, 0 blocked. GitOps.pull returned MergeConflict with `conflict.txt`; direct git reported the same conflicted file and `git merge --abort` succeeded, leaving the repo recoverable.
